headcount_quote: Berkshire and its operating subsidiaries employed approximately 392,400 people worldwide at the end of 2024, of which approximately 80% were in the United States and 20% were represented by unions.

roles.role_phrase_raw: Insurance segment employees (GEICO 28,247; General Re 2,226; BH Reinsurance Group 901; other primary ~10,119) — ~41,493 total
roles.confidence_rationale: Insurance segment workforce (GEICO, General Re, BH Reinsurance, BH Primary) is predominantly underwriters, claims adjusters, actuaries, and insurance agents — maps to financial-analysts.
roles.quote: GEICO: approximately 28,247 employees at year-end 2024. Insurance segment total approximately 41,493 per AFS 2024 subsidiary listing.

roles.role_phrase_raw: Railroad segment — BNSF approximately 36,620 employees, ~87% union members
roles.confidence_rationale: BNSF railroad workforce is overwhelmingly transportation operations (engineers, conductors, maintenance-of-way, yard workers). transportation-storage-and-distribution-managers is the closest karpathy label.
roles.quote: As of December 31, 2024, BNSF Railway had approximately 36,500 employees of whom approximately 32,000 are members of a labor union.

roles.role_phrase_raw: Utilities and Energy segment — Berkshire Hathaway Energy ~23,839 employees
roles.confidence_rationale: BHE operates electric utilities, natural gas pipelines, renewables. Workforce is plant operators, engineers, line workers, field technicians. industrial-engineers is the closest available label but does not fully capture line/plant workers.
roles.quote: Berkshire Hathaway Energy approximately 23,839 employees (2024, per AFS 2024 subsidiary listing). Combined Railroad/Utilities/Energy sector: approximately 60,459 employees.

roles.role_phrase_raw: Manufacturing segment — approximately 180,112 employees (Precision Castparts 24,594; Clayton Homes 20,645; and others)
roles.confidence_rationale: Manufacturing segment (~46% of total workforce) includes Precision Castparts, Clayton Homes, Marmon Group, Forest River, and many others. Workers are primarily production/manufacturing workers and industrial engineers. industrial-engineers is closest label but manufacturing production workers are a large fraction.
roles.quote: Manufacturing sector: approximately 180,112 employees per AFS 2024 subsidiary listing. Includes Precision Castparts (24,594), Clayton Homes (20,645), and numerous other manufacturing subsidiaries.

roles.role_phrase_raw: Service and Retailing segment — approximately 110,305 employees (McLane ~20,000+; Pilot Flying J ~24,000+; other retail/service)
roles.confidence_rationale: Service and Retailing segment includes McLane (food/grocery distribution), Pilot Flying J travel centers, NetJets, Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices, and retail/service businesses. Workforce is predominantly retail associates, distribution/warehouse workers, and service personnel. sales-managers is closest available label.
roles.quote: Service & Retailing sector: approximately 110,305 employees per AFS 2024 subsidiary listing. McLane: more than 20,000 employees; Pilot Flying J: more than 24,000 Team Members.

research_notes: Source: Berkshire Hathaway FY2024 10-K (filed February 2025, CIK 0001067983) plus AFS 2024 supplement. The 10-K body only discloses aggregate ~392,400 total employees, ~80% US, ~20% union. Segment employee counts come from the separately published Annual Filing Supplement (AFS). FY2025 10-K (filed ~March 2026) reports ~387,800 employees but no AFS segment detail yet available. CONSERVATIVE NOTE: the manufacturing and service/retail mappings carry 'medium' confidence because they require inferring role type from segment name without function-level breakdown — these will not qualify for conservative scoring (requires narrow/exact only). Insurance and railroad mappings are 'narrow' and will qualify.
